author: @captainbarnes923
text: Thank you for your content and also the civil way that you accept there will be people who disagree at times. I would like to see some of your theories evolve to the next stage of a practical DIY installation, if only as a proof of concept. However, I am acutely aware that you may have a business to run and may not like to do the R&D for others. A generating garden fence, panels of 6 X 6', to fit between existing concrete supports, perhaps powering a conductive ink panel heater, would be my personal interest, but happy to follow your research journey wherever it leads. Happy New Year.

AURGH !!!!  but that's not your problem that is my problem LOL... I love your work ! the surprising results of your cobbled together wind turbine was flat out amazing to me and I need to see that video again to see how you stopped the magnetic breaking that happens !
or the force that has to be over come to spin.... you cured that so easily and I've struggled for years with that !  Thank you ! now if I can figure out how to get youtube to stop helping me , so I can watch what I want to watch instead of the thousands of music videos it always wants me to see (AURGH !)  maybe I can make some progress in my shop ! HA !
Finding "Coils" as you call them is near imposable for most I am sure You might show how to make them from scratch with enamel coated wire wrapped around a PVC pipe... as that is what I am thinking of doing !  and YES the vertical wind turbine is most definitely the way to go here as I have variable winds from all directions and some times they get real strong
like 91mph ! and I want my turbine to be able to withstand that sort of thing  so I will use strong material !.  super glue in freezing weather don't mix ! at least I haven't had much luck with it !

author: @richardsandwell2285
text:
I have really enjoyed this series, wind turbines are strange things, purists will say you are better off with Aerofoil types like smaller versions of the Grid ones because the tip speeds of the blades are many times higher than the actual wind speed, unlike Savonious types which just spin at the same speed has the wind. However, using turbines in back gardens can be difficult because of planning restrictions regarding noise and turbine safety. This is where your rotary designs will win because they can be disguised and indeed are attractive garden ornaments that can produce power with slower speeds and less noise. 
Some people just miss the bigger picture, I haven't though, I am taking everything in.
And if they produce slightly less power which I doubt it will anyway, the fuel-producing it is totally free so who cares if its a couple of watts lower than a commercial one.

Having two half a inch thick 10" diameter plates should do the job, keeping fingers crossed 😂😂😂😂😂😂 love and very warm regards from India Rob Sir.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: I think the criticisms on presumed torque are based on false assumptions.  If the design does not use a commerciall available shaft-driven generator (brushless motor, or automotive alternator) then torque and horsepower requirements for those devices are irrelevant.  Using a bespoke generator and power controller is simply part of the system design -- especially in proof-of-concept and prototyping.  Your rotor and coil demonstrations are large and heavy, compared to what would fit in a car or motor-driven generator, but well within the power and speed of the device.  Flywheel energy storage (or buffering) is really practical in a stationary, variable-output device, but not at all in most vehicle operations -- look at F1 KERS: great for racing, so far unsuitable for passenger cars or the household or industrial use.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely agree mate

author: @seamuscharles9028
text: Robert a number of centrifugal arms could be installed internally with rods that pass through the centrifuge which move the microwave transformers along a rail track close to the rim where magnetized rods radiate from the axle to the rim This allows for little torque initially or in low breezes irons out some of the turbulence and allows for use in strong winds due to a high load

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh that is interesting mate - cheers

replies.author: @seamuscharles9028
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering further thoughts rather than the old T.P ball type centrifuge it could be a spring loaded arm type movement (imagine arm shoulder down hand the weight) which would lay flat on outer rims where it could be housed against rain ect

replies.author: @seamuscharles9028
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering a simpler centrifuge would be forearm and hand which is a spring loaded mounting set half way between axle and rim

replies.author: @seamuscharles9028
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Robert sorry but the magnetic Sun Ray would have to  be stationary

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Well done.  I should also recommend looking at service life and maintenance interval when evaluating economics for power production systems.  Payoff in a decade or so becomes impossible when 5 year maintenance costs as much as purchase, or lifespan is within 8-12 years.  Your practical "toys", if developed to a useful system by the user, would have a tiny purchase price, which actually makes the payoff within reach while ignoring the operations costs of a commercially approved unit.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @danielppps
text: I believe many people misinterpret Rob's videos, which I am likely guilty of doing when I first became a member of his community. Rob isn't about showing how to do something to achieve some specific outcome, Rob is all about inspiration. Showing you how interesting things can be and how little is required to begin exploring something. He's all about exploration and marvel. All about many different paths you can explore. He doesn't want to go through all the journey, show you how to build, characterize, test and commercialize a device, he's just about showing you that the path exists. You are in charge of walking it if you wish.

author: @kenrowe167
text: Hi Rob. You are right to highlight the overblown claims from turbine manufacturers. Even over here in Co. Mayo on the Atlantic seaboard our average annual windspeed is just 6 m/s (Met Eireann figures). So, from your performance curve, the average output power would be just 200W. In Dublin (average 4 m/s) the average output power would be a dismal 50W. With figures in this range it would take maybe 20 years to recover the installation costs in reduced electricity bills. Really, the only way to gain is to build your own!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely mate - and of course they don't want that! but I think what I am showing is how easy and more importantly how cheap it is to build one that works as well if not better

author: @infertilepiggy5667
text:
ive watched videos that i thought were completely useless and gotten inspiration before, the video doesnt even need to be about the same topic, i think its more about seeing someone do something you didnt think of then you think about how it can be used in your own way, these videos may have ''toys'' in them but its more about the different ways in which you look at things casue after all we all have different life experiences and we dont know everything individually but we know an absurd amount together and if you show someone a concept through a toy then they might take that and go oh yeah i can add this to it or i can just take the base concept and reinvent it in a completely different form mixed with other ideas 

you're doing a fine job anyways rob

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure mate and thank you for taking the time to post an awesome comment

author: @binmanblog
text: Great analysis between cost per watt and "claimed" efficiency". What you say is logical and most people don't have the space for a 4 meter wind turbine on a hillside so exploring low torque vertical axis machines makes sense. Don't be put off by someone who lacks the vision to explore ideas and know that people that follow your channel appreciate your experiments and hope you find an investor to produce a custom model using premium parts.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate but I really am not looking for an investor I am hoping to help folks build their. own and if they want to use the ideas to start their own business everything on here is now public domain and free to use

author: @scotttovey
text:
Sorry Rob but eventually we all have to learn our limitations and I have yet to come across a human being that is able to cure stupid.

I know that you may feel bad that you have to let stupid people remain stupid but; it's just a fact of life that the more intelligent ones must learn to live with as no one has yet come up with a pill that turns stupid people into intelligent people.

author: @emilee172
text: ALL true inventors are NOT or have not have a high education in terms of ALL the laws, those people that have been taught that you cant do that because of the 'law' that stops it put themselves in a box,, so the person that made that comment most likely was taught all the laws that prohibit that it wont work, whereas those that dont know about all the man made laws are the ones that come up with a device that others would have said was impossible, Then those that have more education are the ones that tweak and improve on what was discovered  , So many people are turned off by those that think they know everything and discourage those that are or could be the next big discovery, you think Edison had ALL these degrees in electrical work? Nope he just kept trying and improving on his ideas, SAME with ALL the famous inventors, take Einstein, for a while people said he was retarded, boy were they wrong

author: @dgpreston5593
text:
Hi Rob,
Remember your magnetic gear project? A 1:2 or 1:4 speedup would really get the tip velocity up there.
If torque is not the primary concern, then perhaps a moderate mass flywheel, driving a low mass magnetically-coupled multiplier, would be of interest.

replies.text: that's good thinking mate - I like that - I will look into it

author: @twestgard2
text: Please be aware that there are people who will make rude or angry comments just to see if they can make you react. Sad as it is, it’s a much more popular “hobby” than psychologically healthy people would expect. Do your thing obviously, but just be aware that sometimes these overwrought comments from the public aren’t even the individual’s sincere feelings about the situation.

author: @scantrain5007
text:
Well spoken, Rob!
A wind turbine might be useless in a warm calm summer day, but comes up in windy autum. And ... as West Scotland coast get a 'little bit' more wind as in Cornwal. This is touching the installation of solar pannels as well. - All depend where is the location and how to use this stuff.
What I love in this channel is, that you, dear Rob, brought up a lot of ideas ant hints. For me it started with the item supercaps, gone further how to make own batteries and now the simple way to create wind turbines. 
All this gives possibility to exchange knowletge and expieriences between viewers and you. 
For myself spoken: Your videos became to me as a kind of libary. (How is working a cap? Ask Rob!)
So carry on, I will stay as member as well!

author: @Scott_C
text:
Trolls are going to always be unhappy.  The comment of "all you build are toys" probably stems from the person looking for a specific answer and not getting it. They're frustrated.  
Now, whenever someone has a  complaint like that I try to see their side.  They probably want a "goal" or something that feels like an accomplishment. 

We can set a goal like charging a 100Kw battery, or enough energy to charge a cell phone. Or whatever you may have for each project. Something to give that fulfilment that was lacking.

replies.author: @Scott_C
replies.text: Generating enough power to run that light panel for an hour or two may be a great goal.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: spot on mate - I like the way you see things

replies.author: @Scott_C
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Thanks.  I actually really like the idea of a "goal" for each project.  Illuminating the light panel for an hour or so constantly would present a few good lessons I think anyone who might try to replicate this project might need to learn.

Robert, I sincerely appreciate your efforts and value the results.
I do have some insights and ideas which you may find useful and compelling.
If this is of interest to you, please advise regarding best venue for submittal.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can always write to me - or share them here - as you know I read the comments - it's best if you add them as a new comment to a new video if the comments or videos get more than 3 days old I tend not see a comment as I have just so many comments, emails and posts to reply to and these days I spend about 3 hours a day replying and that's pretty much all I can manage - cheers

author: @delaadland3219
text: Storage: every time we transfer energy and store it we lose efficiency. So the closer to the end use that we harness that energy to use the better? Could your wind energy be used to efficiently heat household hot water for heating or regular hot water use or to supplement hot water use? Any thoughts?

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text: sure, his paint on heaters using his v2 ink apparently works well after watering it down (its to good directly out the tin lol) at 24/32v  good for an easy 100C 200C with aircrete (throw some v2 in the mix) and other insulation, and oc  generic 12v/24v 600watt water tank heating elements are readily available for cheap, basically 300watts per included element

replies.author: @hippie-io7225
replies.text: This low-power generation becomes even more relevant as used storage batteries become less expensive. I bought 3Kw of used LiOn battery for about $250 US. We can think of Rob's wind generator as a "trickle charger".  Once charged, 3Kw can power my fridge during an emergency, for several hours. Low duty cycle charging can really add up!! And paul maydaynight......... putting that energy directly into heat is a GREAT idea!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah I would think so mate - just tie the output to a large heating unit - I like the idea and I will give it a go

replies.author: @paulmaydaynight9925
replies.text:
@hippie-io7225 or paint some rms A4 paper etc sized 500 ream flat/shaped batteries oc, the guys gotta eat in the cold sea swept winter lol, remember we are all locked down again officially from traitors day,5th nov.
interestingly 'the fridge' is an anomaly, or rather the compressor part in that the high power 240v parts are everywhere as scrap and yet expensive to buy new.... 
And the 12/24v versions are cheaper to make at scale,more efficient and yet far to expensive to buy retail,more so than even new 240v, overpriced, overvalued ,cha-ching, No Sale

that's why small diy rhombic drive stirling engines running in 'driven cold mode' are worth simplifying and using the cold end directly today.

oc the advantage of simply painting your heating everywhere as wanted, is you can paint All the pipes and rads directly and do the 'and' thing, heat all the water at every point as required in the loop from different set and forget local low power generators and local diy painted storage for the creative minded.

replies.author: @hippie-io7225
replies.text: @paulmaydaynight9925 I am very interested in better ways to cool... as well.

author: @Buzzhumma
text: If you are serious about building a wind generator of similar style i can highly recommend an E-bike hub motor . I made a few 10 years ago as a vertical wind generator and they work incredibly well . The build design can be experimented with very easy . I wish i had  a youtube channel back then as i would have done some content  but you can get hub motors very cheap and if you get 48 volt version you can make 12 volts at one quarter of the rpm and they are vertially maintainence free  and  inherently highly effecient by design as ebike motor need to be !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: interning stuff mate - I will have a look online for one

replies.author: @Buzzhumma
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering yeah it ticks all the boxes . Mass produced and proven reliability . Some of the lower wattage , like 24 volt 180 watt actually have planetary gearboxes if that can work for your design !

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Rob, you don't need subscribers who view your creations as toys. What you create are prototypes that will provide a proof of concept. To make these prototypes you don't want to spend a lot of money or make it like a final product, you want to get an idea of capabilities and what parts can be improved upon.
Please do not let people like this dishearten you. The only way you will lose my subscription is by my death, you are a very inspiring and motivating person to me and many others. Your insightful nature helps many people on a daily basis, I am very fortunate to have found your videos which have grown to allow me to know you through emails to a point I can call you a dear friend. Thank you and God bless.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: God bless you too mate - and to be honest - I don't for me it's about taking the negative and making a positive out of it - the basic turn a frown upside down lol

replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering you are the man who turns negatives into positives. You're channel is so unique in the YouTube world. I wish I had a mentor like you growing up.
